name: Alcalá La Vieja y cima del Ecce-Homo – circular por el Parque Natural Los Cerros
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros traverse a landscape characterized by limestone hills, extensive pine forests, and riverine vegetation along the Henares riverbank. The park spans over 800 hectares, offering diverse natural features for outdoor exploration. Hikers can find trails winding through dry streams and ascending to prominent points like Mount Ecce Homo, which reaches 836 meters. The terrain provides varied options for easy walks amidst natural and historical elements. Best easy hiking trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros The most popular easy hiking route is Henares River – Tabla Pintora Pine Forest loop from Parque Natural, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This route features river views and sections through pine forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Parque Natural Los Cerros de Alcalá, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This trail offers a circuit through the park's forested areas, with an elevation gain of approximately 97 meters. Local hikers also love the Descent to Cerro Malvecino – Malvecino Summit loop from Parque Natural, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain including hills, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros is defined by limestone hills, pine forests, and the Henares River, with trails that often include historical sites like the ruins of an Islamic fortress. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a significant number of easy hiking trails. The routes in Parque Natural Los Cerros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 150 reviews. More than 1,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Parque Natural Los Cerros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Parque Natural Los Cerros offers a wide selection of routes, with over 80 easy hiking trails available. These trails are designed to be accessible, allowing you to explore the park's diverse landscapes comfortably.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Yes, many easy trails are suitable for families. The park's varied terrain, including pine forests and riverbanks, provides engaging walks for all ages. The Ruta de los Tarayes, mentioned in region research, is particularly suitable for children, showcasing natural and human-influenced environments along the Henares River.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails?
A: Parque Natural Los Cerros is generally dog-friendly, making it a great place to hike with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in the park?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros are circular, offering convenient loop options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23750920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Parque Natural Los Cerros de Alcalá&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3-mile path that takes you through forested areas, providing a complete circuit back to your starting point.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Easy hikes in Parque Natural Los Cerros feature a mix of terrain, including paths through extensive pine forests, along dry streams, and beside the Henares riverbank. You'll also encounter gentle limestone hills, offering varied scenery without significant challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19815959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Cerro Malvecino – Malvecino Summit loop from Parque Natural&lt;/a&gt; includes varied terrain and hills, suitable for an easy hike.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see on easy trails?
A: The park is rich in history. You can explore fascinating elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, an Islamic fortress built in the 9th century, offering historical insights and scenic views. Additionally, some trails pass by caves, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gigantones Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which has historical significance.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints accessible on easy hikes?
A: Absolutely. While Mount Ecce Homo is the highest point, many easy trails offer beautiful vistas. You can find elevated spots within the pine forests and along the hills that provide tranquil views of the surrounding natural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henares River – Tabla Pintora Pine Forest loop from Parque Natural&lt;/a&gt; offers river views and picturesque sections through pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go for an easy hike in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: The park is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for easy hikes. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant, with blooming flora in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable then.

Q: Is there parking available near the easy trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near the main access points to Parque Natural Los Cerros, making it convenient for visitors arriving by car. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked near popular trailheads.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the park's accessibility, the variety of landscapes from riverbanks to pine forests, and the opportunity to combine nature with historical exploration. Many appreciate the well-maintained paths suitable for all skill levels, especially for easy walks.

Q: Can I access the easy trails using public transport?
A: Parque Natural Los Cerros is located near Alcalá de Henares, which has public transport connections. While direct access to every trailhead might vary, you can typically reach the vicinity of the park by bus or train to Alcalá de Henares, and then either walk or take a short local connection to the main park entrances and trailheads.

Q: Are there any shorter easy loops suitable for a quick walk?
A: Yes, there are several shorter easy loops perfect for a quick walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de los Tarayes – Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Polígono Estela II&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.7-mile (2.8 km) route, ideal for a shorter, refreshing outing.
tips: The hill of Ecce Homo or Vera Cruz is located in the natural park of the Hills in Alcalá de Henares, Spain. It has a tabular shape. The climb is quite rough, but safe for hiking or biking. Its slopes are quite steep.

Cave on the slope of Ecce Homo hill. This cave is called "de los Gigantones" because legend has it that ojarancos inhabited it: creatures from Castilian mythology giants with one eye and hair like wild boar bristles. It is also known as "del Champión" since it was used for its cultivation. During the Civil War, it housed several family members who sought protection away from the urban nucleus.

Alcalá la Vieja is the name given to the Arab fortress al-Qal'at abd al-Salam after the recovery of the territory by the Archbishop of Toledo in 1118. The term "the fortress" in Arabic (al-Qal'at ) is what today gives its name to the modern city (built around the Burgo de Santiuste, on the right bank of the river).

It is part of the set of sites that can be visited in the Community of Madrid, although it is currently fenced off and you have to settle for seeing it from the fence.
Easily accessible cave at the base of the Malvecino hill. The main structure is L-shaped, although there are two additional smaller corridors on the sides.
It is a small hill but with steep slopes that must be climbed and descended with great care.
